description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses python:3.7 as the default Docker image and defines three stages: test, deploy, and publish. In the test stage add a test job that exports DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:@postgres:5432/python-test-app, updates apt-get, installs python-dev and python-pip, runs pip install -r requirements.txt && pip install -r requirements-dev.txt, executes coverage run --branch --source=. ./manage.py test, then coverage html and coverage report, and stores the htmlcov/ directory as an artifact that expires after 1 hour while capturing coverage with the regex /TOTAL.+ ([0-9]{1,3}%)/. In the publish stage add a pages job that overrides the image to node:latest, caches node_modules/, runs npm install -g vuepress and cd docs && vuepress build, publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact, and is limited to the master branch. In the deploy stage add a production job that runs only on master, updates apt-get, installs ruby-dev, installs the dpl gem, builds a source distribution with python setup.py sdist, and deploys it to PyPI using dpl --provider=pypi --user=$PIP_USERNAME --password=$PIP_PASSWORD --skip_existing=true.
